news.title: In the news: AAE's Dumbacher quoted in New York Times story on recent New Glenn launch (subscription required)
news.summary_en: Although NASA played no role in the recent New Glenn mission, it is counting on Blue Origin to support the Artemis moon program.

news.title: April Edition: Newly Issued Patents
news.summary_en: Congratulations to the Purdue Engineers who recently received patents on their intellectual property from the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office.

news.title: In the news: AAE's Frueh tells TIME about the looming risk of too many satellites and debris in space
news.summary_en: According to NASA’s Orbital Debris Program Office, more than 25,000 objects larger than 10 cm are currently orbiting Earth, most of them satellites—either active or no longer operating. But that’s only a tiny fraction of the problem. There are 500,000 objects measuring 1 to 10 cm, and 100 million in the 1-mm range.

news.title: ECE's Z. Chen authors 'Birck Builds the Future: Celebrating Twenty Years at Purdue University, 2005-2025'
news.summary_en: Zhihong Chen, a Reilly Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and the Mary Jo and Robert L. Kirk Director of the Birck Nanotechnology Center, and Angie Klink (BA communication '81) published their book 'Birck Builds the Future: Celebrating Twenty Years at Purdue University, 2005-2025.'

news.title: Purdue announces SpaceX team as inaugural laureate of the Neil Armstrong Space Prize
news.summary_en: The 2026 Neil Armstrong Space Prize was announced Tuesday (April 21) at Purdue University, heralding the Falcon 9 Booster Landing Team for their cutting-edge work on the Falcon 9 reusable two-stage rocket system.
